title: Chomsky Hierarchy of Formal Languages
description: A visual guide to the nested hierarchy of formal languages, their corresponding automata, and generative grammars, illustrating the relationship between language complexity and computational power.
relationships: Each level is a strict subset of the level below it (e.g., all Regular languages are Context-Free). Each level is associated with a specific type of automaton (Finite Automata, Pushdown Automata, Linear-Bounded Automata, Turing Machine) and a grammar type that can generate it.
